For non-parametric models, no computationally feasible certificate method exists in our framework. The theory should honestly state: certificates are only applicable when the Gaussian assumption is reasonable.

limitations.name: Within-MEC diversity is causally meaningless
limitations.description: DAGs in the same MEC are observationally indistinguishable (Verma & Pearl 1990). Archive diversity counts only if it spans multiple MECs. The theory cannot prove this happens in practice — it is an empirical question (tested via ES Predictions 1 and 5). This is RT's fundamental objection: 'CausalQD offers maximum diversity of representations while providing minimum diversity of conclusions.'

limitations.name: Faithfulness violations degrade all guarantees
limitations.description: For near-unfaithful distributions (small α), MEC separation (Theorem 1) requires N = Ω(1/α²) samples, which may be infeasible. Strong faithfulness is violated on a set of Lebesgue measure Ω(α) in parameter space (Uhler et al. 2013). For α = 0.05, approximately 5% of parameter space is problematic.

limitations.name: NP-hardness of optimal DAG search
limitations.description: Within-cell optimality (Theorem 5) is asymptotic; the rate constant p_c* is exponentially small in n. Finding the BIC-optimal DAG is NP-hard in general (Chickering et al. 2004). The archive contains locally optimal DAGs, not globally optimal ones. No polynomial-time algorithm can guarantee finding G_c* unless P = NP.

limitations.name: Certificates reduce to known F-tests
limitations.description: The certificate framework (Theorem 2) does not introduce novel statistics; it reframes standard partial F-test / regression significance testing in the QD context. Practitioners familiar with regression will recognize the certificates as standard edge significance tests with a new name.

limitations.name: CausalQD's advantage over Order MCMC is conjectured, not proved
limitations.description: The theory does not formally establish that QD exploration discovers MECs that Bayesian posterior sampling misses (Conjecture 1). AD's proposed comparison theorem was demoted because the required assumptions (uniform cell-filling, equal attractor basins) are unrealistic. Whether CausalQD outperforms Order MCMC is an empirical question.

limitations.name: Scalability constraints
limitations.description: Full algorithm feasible for n ≤ 20 (~18 min per run). Skeleton-restricted for 20 < n ≤ 35. Not recommended for n > 50. The CI signature descriptor is infeasible for n > 20 (O(n⁵)); replaced by MI profile summaries (O(n³ + n²·d_max³)). For n=50, even MI summaries take ~3 hours per run.

limitations.name: Descriptor design is heuristic
limitations.description: No theoretical guarantee that the chosen 13-dimensional descriptors are optimal or even non-degenerate (Conjecture 2). The MI profile may map distinct MECs to the same cell if the corresponding CI differences are along low-variance PCA directions. Descriptor design is a heuristic engineering choice validated empirically, not a theoretically justified selection.

corollaries.name: Archive Fill Time for Polynomial Archives
corollaries.label: cor:poly_fill
corollaries.statement: If K = Θ(n^d) for fixed d > 0, then T_fill(K, δ) = Ω(n^d log n). Linear K=Θ(n) → Ω(n log n); Quadratic K=Θ(n²) → Ω(n² log n); Cubic K=Θ(n³) → Ω(n³ log n); Quartic K=Θ(n⁴) → Ω(n⁴ log n).
corollaries.design_implication: Archive resolution (K) directly determines minimum computational budget. Increasing from cubic to quartic multiplies filling time by Θ(n).

corollaries.name: Local Optima Bound Under Diminishing Returns
corollaries.label: cor:diminishing_returns
corollaries.statement: Under Proposition 8 plus Assumption NCC: L_n ≤ (k+1)^n. Under diminishing returns, each locally optimal parent set is a greedy prefix, giving at most k+1 prefixes per node.
corollaries.proof_sketch: NCC implies s_i has diminishing returns. Local optima must be greedy prefixes. Product over n nodes gives (k+1)^n.
corollaries.numerical_example: For n=10, k=3: L_10 ≤ 4^10 = 1,048,576. Compare to |D_10| > 4×10¹⁸ (exponentially small fraction).
